g.data-narration: Welcome to Software Quality Models - a systematic framework for defining and measuring software quality attributes.
Software quality is the degree to which software meets specified requirements and user expectations, encompassing both internal and external attributes.
g.text: • Degree to which software meets specified requirements

g.data-narration: Quality models help identify defects early - fixing bugs in requirements costs 10x less than in implementation, 100x less than post-deployment.
McCall's model (1977) was one of the earliest comprehensive quality frameworks, defining 11 quality factors organized into three categories.
Product Operation factors define how the software performs: correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, and usability.
Revision factors cover maintainability, flexibility, and testability. Transition factors include portability, reusability, and interoperability.
ISO 9126 defines six main quality characteristics: Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, and Portability.
g.text: International Standard for software quality evaluation

g.data-narration: Each ISO 9126 characteristic has sub-characteristics. For example, Functionality includes suitability, accuracy, interoperability, security, and compliance.
Maintainability includes analyzability, changeability, stability, and testability. Portability covers adaptability, installability, co-existence, and replaceability.
g.text: 📌 ISO/IEC 25010:2011 replaced ISO 9126 with 8 characteristics including Security

g.data-narration: ISO/IEC 25010:2011 is the current standard with 8 characteristics: adds Security, splits Compatibility from Efficiency.
CMMI is a process improvement model with five maturity levels. It focuses on organizational process quality rather than product attributes.
g.text: Process improvement framework - focuses on ORGANIZATIONAL PROCESS quality

g.data-narration: CMMI Level 1 is ad-hoc, Level 2 has basic project management, Level 3 has documented standards, Level 4 uses quantitative techniques, Level 5 focuses on continuous improvement.
Quality metrics include defect density (defects per KLOC) and test coverage. Defect density of 5-15 per KLOC indicates mature organizations.
Compare the models: McCall (11 factors), ISO 9126 (6 chars), ISO 25010 (8 chars), CMMI (5 levels). Product vs Process focus differs.
Remember: McCall focuses on product quality with 11 factors. ISO models measure product quality with sub-characteristics. CMMI measures process maturity. Use defect density formula for metrics.
g.text: 📝 Key: McCall = Product (11), ISO 9126/25010 = Product (6/8), CMMI = Process (5 levels), Defect Density = (D/LOC)×1000
